feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Integration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Databricks DB issue SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on Interface to poll the Data from Databricks(Azure) DB and the connection work fine .. SAP PO JDBC sender adapter is able to fetch the data .. We also executed a simple Query SELECT TABLENAME and it also works fine.. But the issue is when executing a big Query statement...

When the Query statement is directly executed on Azure Databricks DB and it works fine..

Please see below screenshot where it shows the results when executed on Databricks DB..

But JDBC sender channel throws error issue when trying to execute a big Query statement.

Please see below screenshot where it shows the results when executed on Databricks DB.

 

Could you please suggest some inputs to resolve this issue?

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: SAP PI/PO (If Message enter in SAP PI system how message will further Proceed)?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I have a question. One of my interviewers asked me, "The message has entered in your SAP Pi system. What will you do?" like what will we further process.

I'm new to this field, so I don't understand the question. Could you please help me figure out what response he was expecting from me?

I told him that if the target side wants a message of this type, we will map it accordingly and move forward with the message. Was this the right answer?

Thanks,

Sanju.

entry.title: URL giving empty response from POSTMAN/CPI but working fine with Browser
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I am trying to use the URL from browser to get the details, it is working as expected(No Authentication Required). But, when trying to get the details from POSTMAN it is showing an empty response [].

Similar is the case with CPI HTTP adapter as well.

Please suggest, where I am going wrong. I tried other methods apart from GET but nothing working.

Thanks,

Nidhi

entry.title: How to handle x-www-form-urlencoded Content-Type header in SAP PI
entry.content:
Hi experts

I have a proxy to REST integration where I have the following header :

Content-Type: application/x-www-formurlencoded

This Integration is to be used to generate an access toke. The Request Body has the client-id and the client-secret. I am using the following blog for this without the dynamic URL part because I am working with a static URL.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/handling-x-www-form-urlencoded-format-using-rest-adapter/ba-p/13387849 

However I am unable to add the java mapping in the ESR because the jar file attached to the blog is not available anymore. This is also the first time I am working with java mapping and I am honestly quite lost. Most tutorials I have come across seem to be using Netweaver Developer Studio which I am unable to download because I do not have a S-user id. I am also not familiar with importing jar files and most sites I have found do not have anything pointing to those available for SAP PI 7.50 which I am using.

So, I was wondering if it is possible to handle this with a UDF? Also if it is, Do I need to create new data types or message types for this? Also, please guide me on how to write the UDF. I am very new to this so I would really appreciate it if someone could provide me a detailed answer or a link to a blog that provides a detailed answer. I have also gone through this blog without success.

Thank you

entry.title: Custom Error handling in UDF, SAP PI/PO
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We currently have a integration in place that processes price files from vendors. During this process, we perform a lookup to identify any changes in price between the data in our ERP system and the newly received price files. If no price difference is detected, an exception is currently being thrown.

As expected, this results in a large number of errors in the PI/PO message monitor, especially given the volume of vendors we work with. I'm seeking a way to modify this functionality so that instead of throwing an error, we can simply log a message such as 'No price changes detected' without registering it as an error in the message monitor. Is this even possible?

if (recs == 0) {
throw new RuntimeException("EXCEPTION ERROR IN PRICE CONDITION FILTER, ZERO RESULT RECORDS ! ");
}

 Any ideas or possible solutions would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks.

entry.title: SOLVED: SAP PO FILE adapter additional file not keeping original file type in MAIL adapter as attach
entry.content:
I have FILE to MAIL scenario. File sender is processing two file FileToMail.mail and FileToMail.csv. The first one serves as mail body and second one as attachment processed by additional files option in file adapter.

The issue is that FileToMail.csv is attaching as FileToMail.xml and i dont know why. I cant find the reason why is it changing its extension from .csv to .xml and tried many things to fix it without success.

Here is my file sender:

and here is my mail receiver:

I upload these two files into ftp

and result is always this. Mail body correctly processed but attachment with wrong extension:

entry.title: how to find "Delete Additional" job in background job processing monitor
entry.content:
We update our PO system to NetWeaver750 SPS29 recently, according to KBA DIGITS, there is a new job "Delete Additional"  in background job processing monitor, but I couldn't find this in our system, please help to point how to find this job?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to Edit/Trim filename in file receiver channel in SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have an SFTP to File based scenario which is passthrough (No ESR mapping involved)

Here we are picking the statement files from SFTP server and placing in File NFS Al11 path we are getting filename as CAMT05X_11551XXX_309XXX_86530XXX_20241030003216391.xml20241030-013813-047.SNL10248D11730252152604557C

We are using ASMA parameter(SFTP Sender Channel) to maintain same file name from sender SFTP to File receiver channel.

Sender SFTP channel

Receiver File channel

We need to trim or remove the .SNL10248D11730252152604557C from the file name and place it in File NFS Al11 path.

Filename is changing dynamically so how can we achieve this??

Can someone please help here?

Thanks,

Vidya.

entry.title: SAP PI Most messages are going into scheduled and few are processing success
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have a schenario SOAP to SOAP.

Most of the messages are in scheduled mode and only few are processing successfully.

Even unable to get log in message log for scheduled messages.

The messages which are in scheduled mode they are not going into error and success after few hours also still they are in scheduled mode only.

around 35k messages are in schedule mode they are not going to error and not going to process and unable to see the message log and nothing is there to see after few hours from 35k few are processed and few got failed so finally 25 k messages are in scheduled and they are stuck.

Please suggest.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Dileep

entry.title: Looping Condition not getting executed in Local Integration Flow
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an issue where the non-xml condition in the Local Integration flow is not working as expected.

I have added the RunCount = 3 in header and it can be seen in the trace logs but still the condition is not executed. The flow is executed only once, but ideally it should be executed 0,1,2(3 times as per CamelLoopIndex count).

Adding the trace screenshot for your reference below -

Please suggest what is wrong here and why is it not getting executed thrice?

Thanks,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: Integration Flow simulation fails
entry.content:
Hi,

Have an issue with simulation of integration flow. Have tried many times with different logics putting and deleting body/headers/properties, tried from different tenants and browsers and all the time just simple popup 

Does some one know how to fix it or it's global issue?

entry.title: Removed
Behaviour of migrated RFC Sender adapter to HTTPS sender from PO 7.5 to SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing one challenge as below.

From SAP ECC there is a field<SDK_SF>Somevalue</SDK_SF> coming  in payload which was being used in PO through RFC sender adapter in PO message mapping. After migrating to SAP Cloud Integration I am receiving the field from ECC in the payload as <sdksf>Somevalue</sdksf> as the flow is failing at message mapping.

The field is getting changed like underscore and uppercase is getting removed while receiving data from ECC to SAP Cloud Integration.

Is it due to sender RFC or any changes needed in ECC?

Any suggestions will highly be appreciated.

Thanks.

BR,

Purusottam

entry.title: Add delay in Request message SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have Synchronous interface from 3rd party non-SAP to SAP S4 backend system. We have strange requirement, saying whenever there is a request coming from 3rd party system then that request be going to sleep (or) delayed instead of directly sending it to S4 system. Sender 3rd party system should not be receiving any response back to them instead of that nothing should be sent to them.. but at the same time they should not get any response like 500, 404. etc.., however if we go and check in SAP PO monitoring then one should be able to see the messages in monitoring page of SAP PO.. Could you please guide us to achieve this requirement using SAP PO .. thank you!

Regards,

Ramu.

entry.title: Tables and Function modules to build a report using SRT_MONI interface data for sending mail alert
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My requirement is to send an alert (send an email notification) to business users if the interface ended with error status (including all type of errors) available in SRT_MONI. 

The PI (CPI) response back to SAP system was successful and the webservice data has ended into an error due to incorrect data or the data doesn't exist in the SAP system (DB). For example: Company code or Sales organization doesn't exist.

Therefore, business needs notification for these error result interface data along with the Payload data /the original XML file.

To achieve this, we planned to build a report program to list out all the error resulted interface data as an output which are sent as an email notification to the business users (along with the XML file/payload data) information. 

To build the report program, we have the data stored in the table SRT_MMASTER. Using this data we need to fetch or get the Payload information and the XML file details so we can convert into xstring and send it as an attachment file. 

Please share the associated tables and the Function modules to fetch the above required data. 

If you have any other possible solution to send an alert for the above requirement, please let me know and that would be highly appreciated. 

Note:

SXMB_GET_MESSAGE_PAYLOAD  (No result when provided Message ID from the table SRT_MMASTER)

SXMB_GET_MESSAGE_LIST (No result when provided Message ID from the table SRT_MMASTER)

SRT_MONI_GET_CALL_DETAIL (codes are commented and of no use)

Thank you.

entry.title: IN SAP PI/PO there is soap adapter scenario (WSDL)
entry.content: There is scenario of soap adapters and in the receiver side the target wants WSDL file how to do it?

entry.title: Integration Iflow failure calling CPI Tenant API
entry.content:
Hi,

Having next issue, calling SAP CPI Tenant API exactly http://Tenant/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs, first call went fine, next call fails, logic is only filter out per timestamp. Then as my investigation I did next changes in externalized parameter I deleted last slash / and now it again works and again first call is successful and second one fails.

So I'm wondering is there any "Cookies" which somehow are accepted by CPI Iflow and in next amount of time without deleting those cookies we can't call endpoint again?

Response in failure attempts are Status 500 – Internal Server Error

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Is there a way to make a JAR a global resource in CPI like a Script Collection for instance?
entry.content:
Hello community members,

This is the first time I ask a question here so hopefully I'll do that correctly 🙂

My issue is that I need to reuse Groovy classes, that I build for a special purpose, in many different packages containing custom TPM Groovy mappings.  To include the pre-compiled JAR in an IFlow as local ressource is not an issue - this works very well. Today I was searching for a way to make my first JAR available as a global ressource.
The only option that allowed me to upload the library was the CPI Script Collection but this seems to allow classless JARs, containing multiple scripts, only. When I tried to run my mapping with the Script Collection added the test ended with class not found exceptions.

Does anyone know of a way to achieve my request or do I have to wait until SAP offers a way to make JARs globally available in the CPI ecosystem?

Best regards,
Mathias

entry.title: Messages in HOLDING status SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have many messages stuck on SAP PO where receiver system is also SAP PI/PO..Also nothing shows up in message logs and Could you please guide to resolve the issue, Please see below snippets..thanks!

 

 

 

Regards

Ramu

entry.title: Streamlining Business Processes with B2B Integration Factory
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business world, effective communication and efficient data exchange between business partners are crucial. The B2B Integration Factory, a powerful solution designed to simplify and streamline B2B interactions, can transform your business processes. 

In one of our recent projects, I delved deep into the possibilities of the B2B Integration Factory and discovered some insights. At first, it can feel like a lot to take in. But once you get the hang of it, you'll see how useful it can be.  

What is B2B Integration Factory? 

The B2B Integration Factory is a service within SAP that enables organizations to manage their B2B processes more efficiently.

The Partner Directory offers centralized partner management, enabling you to store and manage all partner-specific configurations in one place. This ensures consistency and simplifies the maintenance of partner data. 

Additionally, the B2B Integration Factory provides a wide range of predefined integration content, including mappings, schemas, and connectivity options, which significantly reduce implementation time and effort. 

It is built to handle high transaction volumes and can effortlessly scale to meet your business's growing needs. It supports a variety of sender and receiver communication protocols, including AS2, SFTP, HTTP, and also custom options. 

Last but not least, the B2B Integration Factory ensures secure data exchange with features like encryption, digital signatures, and compliance with industry standards such as EDIFACT, X12, TRADACOMS and more 

 Conclusion 

The B2B Integration Factory in SAP makes B2B interactions easier and more efficient. It cuts down on manual work, reduces mistakes, and speeds up transactions. By using ready-made content, it saves on setup and maintenance costs. Plus, with real-time monitoring and analytics, businesses can keep track of data exchanges and stay competitive in today’s connected world. 

If you would like to learn more, please Contact Proclus - www.proclus-group.com
